mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, the tight labor market forces firms to raise wages and expand hiring, which directly boosts consumer spending and drives positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or neutral forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in housing starts and industrial production indicate synchronized real-economy expansion, which validates corporate revenue growth expectations and lifts the S&P 500 over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over any 3-year rolling window, or the cross-sectional correlation between the proxies and forward returns drops below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with elevated federal funds rates compress real yields and trigger valuation multiple contraction, particularly hurting growth-oriented equities and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns in high-inflation/tightening periods are not statistically lower than baseline (p > 0.05), or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a positive coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales directly reflects strengthening consumer demand, which leads to immediate upward revisions in near-term earnings estimates and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month Spearman rank correlation between retail sales growth and 5-day forward returns remains below 0.05, indicating no predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index exceeding 1.0 percent signals rapid capacity utilization recovery, which drives cyclical sector outperformance and lifts the broader equity index over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances over a 10-year backtest, or the median forward return fails to exceed the unconditional mean by 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous non-farm payroll growth exceeding 150,000 and a declining unemployment rate confirm structural labor market tightening, which sustains wage-driven consumer demand and supports positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint indicator fails to produce a positive information ratio above 0.2 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns in confirmed tightening periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Diverging quarterly GDP growth and declining housing starts indicate structural economic bottlenecks and weakening consumer confidence, which cap corporate earnings upside and generate negative S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive forward returns in more than 50% of instances over a 5-year out-of-sample test, or the cross-sectional predictive power drops below 0.02 Rank IC.
